nodes.label: P06_T001
nodes.identity_summary: A curious, experience-driven consumer who prioritizes friend recommendations above all else but maintains low brand loyalty, constantly seeking new products to try. They value natural/Ayurvedic ingredients and fragrance quality highly, willing to pay ₹350-500 for products that enhance their twice-daily self-care ritual. Their purchase decisions are driven by ingredient transparency and personal recommendations rather than marketing claims or brand prestige.

nodes.label: friend-recommendation-supremacist
nodes.description: Rates friends and family as 5/5 trust source, consistently chooses friend recommendations over expert opinions, dermatologist advice, or marketing claims.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Friends and family: 5, Dermatologists or skin doctors: 3
[REAL] Tried because friend's recommendation — worked for them
[SYNTHETIC] Friend recommendations are my top trigger for trying new body wash brands
[SYNTHETIC] I trust friends and family the most for product recommendations
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely ignore brand claims and expert endorsements but immediately try it if a close friend mentioned using it successfully.

nodes.label: low-loyalty-constant-experimenter
nodes.description: Explicitly states low brand loyalty and demonstrates consistent willingness to try new brands, switching frequently based on curiosity rather than dissatisfaction.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Low_loyalty
[REAL] Curious Fun Optimistic
[REAL] How comfortable are you trying new brands: 5
[SYNTHETIC] Different brands each time
nodes.predictive_value: When shown multiple body wash options, this person would likely gravitate toward unfamiliar brands or new variants rather than sticking with established choices.

nodes.label: ingredient-transparency-seeker
nodes.description: Wants detailed information about product formulations and ingredients, particularly preferring natural or fruit-based components.
evidence_quotes: [SURVEY] 'I'd want more transparency about the specific ingredients used in the custom formulations'
[SURVEY] 'especially ensuring they include natural or fruit-based components I prefer'
[SURVEY] Shows interest in 'micellar technology' innovation
[SURVEY] Evaluates product claims as 'Somewhat believable' consistently, suggesting need for more information
nodes.predictive_value: When shown product concepts, this person would likely seek detailed ingredient information and respond more positively to products that highlight natural or specific beneficial ingredients.

nodes.label: ayurvedic-wisdom-preferrer
nodes.description: Shows strong preference for Ayurvedic products over clinical claims, rating traditional wisdom higher than modern dermatological approaches.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Strong_ayurvedic (when asked clinical vs Ayurvedic)
[REAL] Ayurvedic,ph_balanced,sulfate_free,cruelty_free,organic,dermatologist_tested,paraben_free
[SYNTHETIC] I strongly prefer Ayurvedic wisdom over clinical claims
[SYNTHETIC] Ancient wisdom and natural ingredients matter more to me than clinical claims
nodes.predictive_value: When shown competing body wash concepts, this person would likely choose one positioned with Ayurvedic heritage and traditional ingredients over one with clinical testing claims.

nodes.label: fragrance-experience-maximizer
nodes.description: Considers fragrance non-negotiable, wants all-day lasting scent, and specifically loves ocean/aquatic fragrances that enhance the shower experience.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Ocean_aquatic,floral (preferred fragrances)
[REAL] All_day (how long fragrance should last)
[REAL] Lux (ocean variant) - Yes, fresh sea breeze
[SYNTHETIC] Fragrance is non-negotiable in body wash
nodes.predictive_value: When shown a new body wash concept, this person would likely focus immediately on fragrance description and longevity claims, especially responding to ocean/aquatic scent profiles.

nodes.label: self-care-ritual-investor
nodes.description: Views bathing as a self-care ritual, showers twice daily, and is willing to pay ₹350-500 for products that enhance the experience with good foam and ambience.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Self_care_ritual
[REAL] Twice (daily showers)
[REAL] Better ambience with sound and light (ideal routine)
[REAL] 350_500 (typical spend)
nodes.predictive_value: When shown premium body wash concepts, this person would likely justify higher prices if the product promises to enhance their daily ritual experience with superior texture, foam, or sensory elements.

nodes.label: quick-commerce-convenience-adopter
nodes.description: Regularly uses quick commerce platforms like Blinkit/Zepto and makes purchase decisions before opening apps, valuing instant delivery over extensive browsing.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] quick_commerce,nykaa,flipkart (top purchase channels)
[REAL] Yes, but limited selection doesn't change choices — decide before opening the app
[SYNTHETIC] I decide before opening the app anyway
[SYNTHETIC] Order from Blinkit and get it delivered in 15 minutes
nodes.predictive_value: When needing body wash urgently, this person would likely choose quick commerce delivery over waiting for better prices or selection, having already decided what type of product they want.

nodes.label: youtube-influencer-truster
nodes.description: Rates YouTube influencers as 5/5 trust source and prefers video content over text reviews, trusting creators who address specific skin problems.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Instagram or YouTube influencers: 5
[REAL] youtube,influencer (where they discover products)
[REAL] trust based on what problem they address
[SYNTHETIC] I trust influencers and video content more than text reviews
nodes.predictive_value: When researching a new body wash, this person would likely seek out YouTube reviews from creators discussing specific skin concerns rather than reading text-based reviews or brand descriptions.

nodes.label: price-conscious-value-seeker
nodes.description: As a student with 1-2L household income, consistently chooses lower-priced options and is motivated by discounts, but willing to pay for proven benefits.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] 1l_2l (income range)
[REAL] Student
[REAL] international brands priced higher, so less likely to buy them
[SYNTHETIC] ₹50 savings is significant, and I use body wash regularly
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash options at different price points, this person would likely gravitate toward mid-range Indian brands over premium international ones, but would pay extra for specific benefits like long-lasting fragrance.

nodes.label: foam-texture-essentialist
nodes.description: Considers lather/foam essential to the body wash experience and prefers mousse texture, viewing good foam as non-negotiable for satisfaction.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Essential (importance of lather/foam)
[REAL] Mousse (preferred texture)
[REAL] Fragrance and foam (what they like most)
[REAL] Good smell makes the process enjoyable; take more time with it
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts, this person would likely immediately assess foam quality and texture claims, rejecting products that don't promise rich, satisfying lather regardless of other benefits.

nodes.label: sensitive-skin-accommodator
nodes.description: Has specific skin concerns (dryness, rough texture, sensitivity) and looks for sulfate-free, pH-balanced products while being cautious about irritation.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Dryness,rough_texture,sensitivity
[REAL] sulfate_free (among preferred certifications)
[REAL] ph_balanced (among appealing claims)
[SYNTHETIC] I have sensitive skin as one of my concerns
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new body wash concepts, this person would likely focus on gentle, sulfate-free formulations and would be interested in pH-balanced claims, especially for addressing dryness and sensitivity.

nodes.label: sample-before-commit-strategist
nodes.description: Prefers trying products through samples, sachets, or mini sizes before committing to full bottles, especially for unknown brands or higher prices.
evidence_quotes: [SYNTHETIC] Buy a ₹99 mini size from Nykaa first
[SYNTHETIC] ₹10 is nothing to risk, and I can test if I like the fragrance
[SYNTHETIC] Only try if they offer a 50ml trial size
[SYNTHETIC] The safety net is worth the higher per-ml cost
nodes.predictive_value: When shown premium or unfamiliar body wash concepts, this person would likely seek out trial sizes or samples rather than committing to full bottles, valuing the ability to test fragrance and skin compatibility first.

nodes.label: anti-marketing-authenticity-seeker
nodes.description: Rates brand advertising claims and TV ads very low (1-2/5) while trusting user experiences and ingredient transparency over promotional content.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Claims written on the product packaging: 2, TV advertisements: 1
[REAL] Online reviews (Amazon, Nykaa): 2
[SYNTHETIC] I trust recommendations from people without bias over brand claims
[SYNTHETIC] Real user experiences are more trustworthy than brand clinical claims
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash marketing campaigns, this person would likely be skeptical of brand claims and instead seek out authentic user experiences, ingredient transparency, and unbiased third-party content.

nodes.label: moisturized-skin-outcome-focused
nodes.description: Specifically wants post-wash skin to feel moisturized rather than tight or clean, prioritizing hydrating benefits over cleansing power.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Moisturized (ideal post-wash skin feeling)
[REAL] Dryness (among skin concerns)
[REAL] shea_vitamin_e (among appealing claims)
[SYNTHETIC] I'd want something effective for the colors and don't mind buying for specific occasions
nodes.predictive_value: When shown body wash concepts, this person would likely prioritize moisturizing claims and ingredients like shea butter or vitamin E over deep-cleansing or purifying benefits.

nodes.label: curiosity-driven-experimenter
nodes.description: Describes themselves as curious and consistently shows interest in trying new ingredients, formats, and innovative products even from unknown brands.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] Curious Fun Optimistic
[SYNTHETIC] Fascinated — would research and likely try it
[SYNTHETIC] The mystery element is intriguing
[SYNTHETIC] I'm curious about new ingredients
nodes.predictive_value: When shown innovative body wash concepts with unique ingredients or formats, this person would likely be drawn to try them based on novelty and curiosity rather than proven track records.

nodes.label: platform-loyalty-over-brand-loyalty
nodes.description: Prefers shopping on familiar platforms (Nykaa, Flipkart, Blinkit) over brand websites, even when products are available elsewhere.
evidence_quotes: [REAL] quick_commerce,nykaa,flipkart
[SYNTHETIC] I prefer platforms I trust
[SYNTHETIC] Wait until they're available on Amazon or Nykaa
[SYNTHETIC] I'd rather search for something with similar ocean scent that's available locally
nodes.predictive_value: When shown new body wash brands available only on their own websites, this person would likely wait for the products to appear on established platforms rather than purchasing directly from unfamiliar sites.

nodes.label: natural-packaging-preferrer

[SYNTHETIC] I'm paying for the formula, not the packaging

nodes.label: recommendation-context-analyzer

nodes.label: technology-driven-solution-embracer

[SURVEY] Consistently rates technology-driven concepts as most innovative and appealing

nodes.label: specific-skin-concern-addresser

[SURVEY] 'The 12-hour moisturizing benefit directly addresses my skin dryness concerns'
[SURVEY] 'custom formulation approach aligns perfectly with my preference for personalized products that target my exact skin issues'
[SURVEY] Moisturization rated 5/5 importance, gentleness 4/5
nodes.predictive_value: When shown targeted skincare concepts, this person would likely prioritize products that specifically address dryness and sensitivity over general-purpose body washes.

links.mechanism: High trust in friends (5/5) directly reinforces low trust in brand advertising (1-2/5) because they value authentic personal experiences over promotional content.
Their curious personality drives the willingness to try new brands, making low brand loyalty a natural outcome of their experimental nature.
Need for ingredient information reinforces cautious approach of testing products before full commitment to ensure they meet quality expectations.
Preference for traditional Ayurvedic ingredients reinforces their need to read and understand ingredient lists, especially seeking natural and fruit-based components.
Viewing bathing as a ritual makes fragrance quality crucial for enhancing the experience, justifying investment in all-day lasting scents.
Preference for instant delivery through familiar platforms reinforces trust in established channels over brand-specific websites or unfamiliar purchasing methods.
Trust in influencers is conditional on them addressing specific problems, creating a pattern of evaluating all recommendations based on relevance to personal concerns.
Student budget constraints drive the need to test products through samples before committing to full-size purchases, minimizing financial risk.
Essential need for good foam quality extends to overall texture preferences, making the physical sensory experience a key decision factor.
Having specific skin concerns (dryness, sensitivity) necessitates thorough research to avoid products that might cause irritation or worsen conditions.
Desire to try new products conflicts with need to maintain consistent twice-daily self-care routine, requiring backup strategies to manage both impulses.
Willingness to pay premium for lasting fragrance aligns with viewing bathing as a ritual worth investing in for enhanced daily experience.
While they make independent decisions and live alone, they heavily rely on their best friend's input, creating tension between autonomy and peer influence.
Ethical stance on animal testing aligns with preference for traditional, natural approaches over modern clinical testing methods.
Price sensitivity conflicts with willingness to pay premium for lasting fragrance, creating decision tension between value-seeking and experience-maximizing behaviors.
Their willingness to pay premium for personalized solutions reinforces their attraction to technology-enabled customization like AI skin analysis.
Their focus on moisturization drives interest in products that eliminate separate lotion application, combining cleansing and moisturizing into one step.
Desire for lasting fragrance aligns with overall focus on enhanced sensory experiences during and after showering.
Interest in innovative concepts reinforces openness to trying different brands and products rather than sticking to familiar options.
Price sensitivity creates desire for trial options to validate value before making full investment in premium products.
Their need for rich lather and tactile experiences reinforces rejection of convenience-focused products that compromise sensory benefits.
Having specific skin issues like dryness and sensitivity drives willingness to pay more for customized solutions that target exact concerns.
